mnemonics: Greedily grab the best option first
keyPoints: Definition: A heuristic search algorithm that always chooses the node with the highest priority (or best value) first
description: Also known as Best First Search, it explores the node with the highest priority first and continues to explore its neighbors until it finds the goal or exhausts all possibilities.
definitions: Heuristic: A function that estimates the distance from a node to the goal
examples: In a grid-based problem, choose the node with the lowest Manhattan distance to the goal first
cases: In a problem with multiple goals, the algorithm chooses the node with the highest priority (or best value) first
pros: Efficient for problems with a clear heuristic function
cons: May not always find the optimal solution if the heuristic function is poor
